title: Retinal Vascular Permeability Protocol
author: David A. Antonetti
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yagfsbw
materials: Anesthetize the animal again with ketamine/xylazine

steps.title: Weigh animal and record body weight...
steps.instruction: Weigh animal and record body weight for anesthetic and dye injections

steps.title: Make an incision on skin inside...
steps.instruction: Make an incision on skin inside of the hind leg and carefully tear away the membranes to isolate the femoral vein

steps.title: Inject FITC-BSA into the femoral vein...
steps.instruction: Inject FITC-BSA into the femoral vein at 2 µl/g body weight (equal to 200 mg/kg body weight) using a 31-gauge 0.3 c.c. insulin syringe (vortex FITC-BSA before use)

steps.title: Apply pressure on the injection site...
steps.instruction: Apply pressure on the injection site with a sterile gauze or cotton swab to stop bleeding

steps.title: Staple the incision site and allow...
steps.instruction: Staple the incision site and allow FITC-BSA to circulate for 2 hours

steps.title: Open the abdomen and draw 0
steps.instruction: Open the abdomen and draw 0.3 ml blood from the vena cava with a 27-gauge 1 c.c. syringe

steps.title: Remove needle and expel blood into a heparinized micro-cuvette
steps.instruction: Remove needle and expel blood into a heparinized micro-cuvette

steps.title: Mix blood sample by gently reversing...
steps.instruction: Mix blood sample by gently reversing the tube several times and keep on ice

steps.title: Open the chest cavity, cannulate the...
steps.instruction: Open the chest cavity, cannulate the heart with a blunt end 22-gauge needle into the left ventricle and incise right atrium to release pressure

steps.title: Perfuse with saline (warmed to 37°C)...
steps.instruction: Perfuse with saline (warmed to 37°C) at 20 ml/min via the left ventricle for 2 minutes

steps.title: Harvest the retina and place in a pre-weighed microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Harvest the retina and place in a pre-weighed microcentrifuge tube. Rinse the harvest tools between samples to avoid cross contamination

steps.title: Centrifuge the blood sample at 2,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the blood sample at 2,000 x g at 20°C for 15 minutes to separate plasma

steps.title: Transfer the plasma to a new...
steps.instruction: Transfer the plasma to a new microcentrifuge tube and store at -80°C

steps.title: Dry the retina samples with a Speed-Vac overnight
steps.instruction: Dry the retina samples with a Speed-Vac overnight

steps.title: Weigh the microcentrifuge tube containing the...
steps.instruction: Weigh the microcentrifuge tube containing the dry retina to obtain the dry retina weight

steps.title: Add 100 µl of 1% Triton-PBS...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µl of 1% Triton-PBS to each retina and shake overnight to extract FITC-BSA

steps.title: Vortex briefly, centrifuge the microcentrifuge tubes...
steps.instruction: Vortex briefly, centrifuge the microcentrifuge tubes at 17,000 x g for 30 minutes and transfer supernatant to a new microcentrifuge tube

steps.title: Dilute the plasma samples with 1% Triton-PBS
steps.instruction: Dilute the plasma samples with 1% Triton-PBS

steps.title: Dilute the stock FITC-BSA (100 mg/ml)...
steps.instruction: Dilute the stock FITC-BSA (100 mg/ml) with 1% Triton-PBS and make serial dilutions to obtain the FITC-BSA standards

steps.title: Measure the standards (0
steps.instruction: Measure the standards (0.156 to 10 µg/ml), retina extract and diluted (1:900) plasma samples (20 µl/well, triplicate) in a 384-well black/clear bottom plate with a fluorescent plate reader (excitation 488 nm, emission 520 nm)

steps.title: Calculate the FITC-BSA concentration of each...
steps.instruction: Calculate the FITC-BSA concentration of each sample with the standard curve NOTE: The auto-fluorescence background of the retina from animal without FITC-BSA injection should be subtracted for the permeability calculation.Additional tip that improves efficiency.
